name: OPEC+
summary: Day 63 Thursday. UAE OPEC EXIT TAKES EFFECT TOMORROW MAY 1 — CALENDAR INFLECTION ~24 HOURS AWAY; SAUDI-UAE RIFT CRYSTALLIZES AT JEDDAH SUMMIT (UAE FM NOT PRESIDENT); SAUDI 3-DAY CONSPICUOUS-QUIET = 'NO GOOD PUBLIC OPTIONS' THRESHOLD CROSSED. UAE OPEC exit takes effect TOMORROW May 1 — calendar inflection ~24 hours away. Saudi-UAE rift visible at Jeddah Apr 28 GCC summit: UAE sent FM not President (visible diplomatic snub); UAE Energy Minister Al Mazrouei confirmed UAE did NOT consult Saudi Arabia or any other OPEC country before announcement. Saudi former oil adviser al-Sabban via Al Jazeera deflective ('not a major blow'); MBS no direct statement Tue-Wed-Thu — 3-day Saudi conspicuous-quiet crosses threshold from 'considering response' to 'no good response.' US News: 'UAE Oil Break Exposes Deepening Saudi Rift as Gulf Power Shifts.' Atlantic Council: 'Petrodollar Crisis' / 'no good public options.' Soufan Center IntelBrief 'Gulf Shakeup.' With UAE OUT effective May 1, June OPEC+ ministerial decision on production discipline rests on Saudi-Russia-Iraq-Kuwait alone — fundamentally different cartel architecture. Brent closes $118-120 (+6-8% Apr 29, Day 1 of $115-sustained CONFIRMED) priced ~20% UAE-OPEC fragmentation contributor. Atlantic Council/Derentz framing for medium-term: UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity (5M bpd target by 2027) is medium-term-BEARISH-for-Brent (12-24mo) even as today's tape priced near-term-bearish-for-OPEC-coordination = bullish near-term Brent. Goldman late-2026 Brent forecast $90 institutionalizes 'higher for longer.' Saudi-Iraq 'covert war' framing remains active. OPEC overall -27% MoM (28.7 → 20.8 mbpd), Saudi -23% (10.1 → 7.8 mbpd). PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): UAE announced Tuesday April 28 it will leave OPEC and OPEC+ effective May 1, 2026, ending 50+ years of membership (CONFIRMED via The National UAE state media + 7+ Western wires + Atlantic Council/CFR). UAE Energy Minister explicitly: timing 'is now because the Strait of Hormuz is closed and the impact of its exit on the oil market will be limited.' This is structural realignment of Gulf petroleum geopolitics, NOT a quota dispute. CRITICAL BESSENT-CONTEXT: UAE Central Bank Governor Balama negotiated dollar-swap-line provisions with Treasury Secretary Bessent days before — UAE consolidating inside American financial-and-security architecture. Operational implications for OPEC: (a) UAE is second-largest OPEC producer with crucial spare capacity — Saudi Arabia now does more of heavy lifting on price stability; cartel loses key shock absorber, (b) Atlantic Council: UAE absence may pose 'existential risk to OPEC sustainability' over 12-24mo, (c) MBS PUBLIC SILENCE Tue-Wed is itself data (Riyadh's anger; Atlantic Council: Saudi will 'view this action as a new direct threat'), (d) potential UAE-Saudi rift visible in late-2025 Yemen-related tensions. Atlantic Council/Derentz framing: 'Now that the UAE won't be beholden to OPEC production quotas, nothing inhibits the country from leveraging its spare capacity in pursuit of its own strategic interests.' UAE 5M bpd capacity target by 2027 implies 500K-1M bpd additions runway → structural ceiling LOWER 12-24mo out (medium-term-bearish-for-Brent) BUT loss of OPEC shock-absorber is near-term-bearish-for-management (today's tape +3.04% to $114.64 — 8th straight up session, intraday >$115). The split-temporal interpretation explains why today's tape priced near-term-bearish-for-management while equity narrative talked medium-term-bullish-for-supply. June OPEC+ ministerial decision now runs under FUNDAMENTALLY DIFFERENT cartel architecture with UAE outside the room. Goldman late-2026 Brent forecast $90 institutionalizes 'higher for longer' (raised from $80) — but Atlantic Council/Derentz argues UAE-OPEC-exit makes Goldman's path TOO HIGH on the medium-term ceiling. Aramco May OSP +$19.50/bbl premium (Apr 6) record-high but Bloomberg refiner survey expected $40+ — '$20.50 left on the table' = pricing restraint signaling Aramco preparing for partial Hormuz reopening. Saudi-Iraq 'covert war' framing (UNN/WSJ Apr 26) inside OPEC remains active. Structural production suppression: OPEC overall -27% MoM (28.7 → 20.8 mbpd), Saudi -23% (10.1 → 7.8 mbpd). IEA Director Birol: '~13 mbpd disrupted' (vs 1973 ~5, 1979 ~5.7, 1990 ~4.3 mbpd) — 'biggest energy supply shock on record.' OPEC+ Apr 5 206K bpd increase confirmed for May; June ministerial decision now shaped by UAE-departure-architecture rather than crisis-management consensus.

signals.content: OPEC+ agreed to 206K bpd increase for April (debated range 137K-548K bpd). Modest because spare capacity limited and export routes threatened.
Actual OPEC output rose 640K bpd ahead of conflict (biggest increase since June), with Saudi accounting for ~50%. But export routes now blocked.

9 DAYS WITHOUT EMERGENCY MEETING despite Brent at $100-118 — the highest sustained prices since the 2022 Russia shock. OPEC+ agreed only a modest 206K bpd increase (announced pre-war). Saudi activated Yanbu bypass at 4.2M bpd. Most OPEC members' barrels are STRANDED if Hormuz stays shut — even increased production can't reach market. The non-response IS intelligence: (1) Saudi/Gulf members benefit from high prices (revenue) even while suffering from the war (attacks); (2) Production increases are meaningless without export routes; (3) Any emergency meeting would highlight OPEC's impotence while Hormuz is closed. IEA coordinated 400M barrel SPR release filling the gap partially but SPR at 243M bbl (lowest since 1984). (Sources: IEA, OPEC+, DOE, Bloomberg)

CONFIRMED: OPEC+ confirmed 206K bpd increase for April. April 5 meeting (3 days away) will decide May production levels. However, most OPEC+ barrels are stranded — Iraq and Kuwait involuntarily shutting production because they cannot export through Hormuz. Saudi Yanbu bypass (4.2M bpd) is the only functioning Gulf export route, now overwhelmed with ~40 VLCCs anchored outside with 5-day waits. OPEC+ production decisions are partially academic while Hormuz remains disrupted — increased quotas mean nothing when barrels cannot reach market. (Sources: OPEC+, Bloomberg)

April 5 meeting 2 DAYS AWAY — described as 'most consequential since formation.' Planned 206K bpd increase for April (Saudi +62K, Russia +62K). But most barrels stranded behind Hormuz — production increase symbolically important, operationally meaningless. Saudi spare capacity ~3M bpd is the largest buffer. WTI at $112, Brent at $109 — OPEC+ members earning record per-barrel revenue. Incentive structure: high prices benefit producers even while Hormuz limits actual exports. Market expects status quo — maintain planned increase, signal confidence. Any surprise (larger increase, emergency meeting, delay) moves oil $5-10. (Sources: OPEC official, CNBC, Fortune, Bloomberg)

CONFIRMED: OPEC+ approved 206,000 bpd output increase for May — same increment as April. The group explicitly noted this is 'less than 2 percent of the oil supply affected by Hormuz closure' and retained 'full flexibility to increase, pause, or reverse.' Warned: 'restoring damaged energy assets to full capacity is both costly and takes a long time, thereby affecting overall supply availability.' The increase is a PAPER increase — most producers (Saudi, UAE, Kuwait, Iraq) CANNOT deliver because Hormuz is closed. 206K bpd = zero incremental barrels today. The REAL signal: OPEC+ is pre-positioning for post-war world. Unanimous vote (including Russia) means cartel will NOT restrain output once peace arrives. This puts a ceiling on post-ceasefire oil floor — Brent likely drops below $90 faster than market expects if peace arrives.

OPEC+ confirmed 206K bpd increase for May — same as April. Symbolic: <2% of ~17-20M bpd disrupted by Hormuz. Most barrels stranded behind closed/congested channels. Market reads this as READINESS signal to flood post-ceasefire, not current supply relief. Yanbu bypass overwhelmed: 32 tankers queued, port at 4M bpd vs 7M pipeline. Paper increases cannot translate to physical supply until Hormuz reopens. Post-war oil unwind expected FASTER than anticipated because OPEC+ pre-committed. (Sources: CNBC, Fortune, ENR)

OPEC+ April 5 decision aftermath: 206K bpd May increase confirmed. Saudi has been throttling production as storage maxed. The increase is a placeholder — designed to flood markets the moment Hormuz reopens. Bart Melek/TD Securities estimate: nearly 1B barrels lost by end of April if Strait stays effectively shut to Western shipping. Scenario 2 (peace) features faster oil unwind than market anticipates — Brent could fall below $90 within 2 weeks of confirmed Hormuz reopening as OPEC+ supply hits market. Russia voted with the bloc — alignment locked.

EVENING UPDATE: OPEC+ agreed 206K bpd increase for May — Saudi +62K, Russia +62K, Iraq +26K, UAE +18K, Kuwait +16K, Kazakhstan +10K, Algeria +6K, Oman +5K. Bloomberg: contingent on Hormuz reopening. Gulf producers cannot ship through Hormuz. OPEC+ warned 'restoring damaged assets is costly and takes a long time.' Next meeting May 3. Paper decision — cannot be delivered through closed channels. (Sources: BNN Bloomberg, Rigzone, Euronews)

OPEC+ POSITION FUNDAMENTALLY REFRAMED BY CEASEFIRE [REPORTED]. The 14-day Trump-Iran ceasefire transforms OPEC+'s strategic position. Pre-ceasefire: OPEC+ had announced a 206K bpd production increase for May, but this was paper-only because the Yanbu bypass was at capacity (~40 VLCCs anchored 5-day waits) and Hormuz was effectively closed to Western flows. The increase was symbolic, less than 2% of disrupted supply. Post-ceasefire: if Hormuz reopens physically (which Lloyd's notes takes WEEKS not days due to insurance restoration), the 206K bpd CAN actually flow, AND OPEC+ faces a strategic choice — extend the increase, accelerate it, or hold to defend prices. The key tension: Saudi Arabia (the SABIC/Sadara damage forces them to NEED higher revenue) vs Russia (which has been quietly profiting from Iran's three-tier exemption and now faces normalization). OPEC+'s next meeting becomes a pivotal binary: a coordinated production hold defends prices at $95-100; an unwinding race to the bottom collapses Brent toward $80-85. **The ceasefire likely accelerates OPEC+ supply restoration faster than originally expected — meaning Brent's path lower extends if the deal holds**. Goldman's $80 year-end forecast was conditional on 6 weeks of disruption; with the ceasefire 14-day window starting now, $80 by Q3 is now plausible. For the investor, this is the macro mechanism that walks back the recession-via-oil pathway documented in the fed/us-10y-yield nodes.

NO OPEC+ EMERGENCY MEETING ANNOUNCED POST-CEASEFIRE — ABSENCE AS INTELLIGENCE [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. Per intel.md Section E: 'No OPEC+ emergency meeting announced post-ceasefire. The 206K bpd symbolic increase remains the only production commitment.' The cartel has not publicly coordinated a response to the ceasefire fracture within 18 hours. The silence suggests internal disagreement: Saudi (now the worst-positioned Gulf state with Sadara $20B JV destroyed AND Tadawul -1.6% on lower oil revenue + continued strikes on East-West Pipeline pumping station) needs higher prices but cannot publicly resist a US-brokered ceasefire framework; Russia (quietly profiting from Iran's three-tier exemption + April 9 palladium tariff fears) faces a different normalization timeline. Iran's re-closure of Hormuz on Day 1 renders the 206K bpd increase paper-only again — Yanbu at capacity, physical flows blocked. Friday Islamabad outcome is the next OPEC+ catalyst — success forces an accelerated unwind discussion; failure makes Saudi-Russia internal split more acute.

Day 44. SAUDI WAR DAMAGE QUANTIFIED — OPEC+ LEAD PRODUCER STRUCTURALLY CONSTRAINED [CONFIRMED from multiple independent sources]. Saudi Aramco war damage tally: (a) production capacity cut ~600,000 bpd, (b) East-West Pipeline throughput cut ~700,000 bpd, (c) Manifa field capacity reduced 300,000 bpd, (d) SATORP refinery (TotalEnergies JV) SHUT DOWN (TotalEnergies issued official statement), (e) Ras Tanura, Samref, Riyadh refineries also hit, (f) Juaymah LPG/NGL exports curbed, (g) 1 Aramco security staff killed, 7 workers injured. Arab Light OSP for May set at RECORD $19.50/bbl premium over Oman/Dubai — the highest OSP premium ever published by Aramco. This has three structural implications for OPEC+: (1) The 206K bpd May increase is now physically constrained not just politically — Saudi (allocated +62K) cannot deliver its share even if Hormuz reopens. (2) The cartel cannot publicly coordinate a defensive production response because the lead producer is capacity-constrained, not strategically reserving. (3) Goldman's Q2 Brent revision to $90 (from $99) still keeps prices elevated precisely BECAUSE Saudi cannot surge — the supply-side ceiling on Brent downside has been raised by the damage tally. JPMorgan's $150 Brent warning if Hormuz stays shut into mid-May is now <5 weeks away. The cartel's post-ceasefire silence is now understood as STRUCTURAL absence, not strategic absence: there is no coordinated response available. Next OPEC+ meeting May 3. Friday/Saturday Islamabad outcome remains the next catalyst. (Sources: The National Abu Dhabi, Euronews, OilPrice.com, World Oil, TotalEnergies press release)

OPEC OVERALL PRODUCTION -27% MoM (28.7 → 20.8 MBPD); SAUDI -23% (10.1 → 7.8 MBPD) — STRUCTURAL SUPPRESSION CONFIRMED [CONFIRMED structural data via CNBC OPEC report April 13]. OPEC's overall production fell 27% month-over-month from 28.7 mbpd to 20.8 mbpd, reflecting Gulf Arab states' inability to export through the Strait of Hormuz under the IRGC-imposed selective control. Saudi Arabia specifically went from 10.1 to 7.8 mbpd (-23%). April 9 IRGC strike on East-West pipeline cut additional 700,000 bpd capacity that Riyadh announced full restoration of by April 12. With IRGC Wednesday kinetic resumption (MSC Francesca/Epaminondas seizure inside ceasefire envelope), the structural-suppression read HOLDS. OPEC+'s March agreement to raise production by 206,000 bpd is RENDERED COUNTERFACTUAL by physical-export blockage. Implication: global oil supply picture more constrained than headline 'production increase' framing suggests; physical Brent (Dated $131.97 last seen April 10) premium over paper ($99.81) persists even if paper holds $95-100 band.
sources: CNBC 'Middle East oil production plunges due to Iran war, OPEC data shows'
Vision2030 analysis 'Iran War Saudi Economy April 2026'

signals.content: BRANCH 3 RESOLUTION → JUNE MINISTERIAL PRODUCTION-UNWIND SPECULATION ACCELERATES; ARAMCO MAY OSP +$19.50/BBL PREMIUM SIGNALS PRICING RESTRAINT [CONFIRMED + REPORTED]. Brent gapped -4.6% to $100.47 on Sunday Globex / Monday Asia open — Branch 3 (positioning) defeats Branch 1 (structural break) and Branch 2 (theatrical band). Crisis premium compressed $35 → $30 = re-rating of TIME-EXTENSION risk, not LEVEL risk. Aramco May Official Selling Price set at +$19.50/bbl premium over Oman/Dubai (Apr 6 announcement) — RECORD HIGH absolute level, but Bloomberg refiner survey had expected $40+ premium given physical pricing reality. Aramco 'left $20.50 on the table' = explicit pricing restraint signal. Mechanism: Aramco preparing for Hormuz partial reopening / quicker resolution architecture rather than extending crisis premium. Branch 3 environment makes the June OPEC+ ministerial meeting structurally consequential — under Russia-mediated 4-8 week resolution architecture (Putin-Araghchi diplomatic channel confirmed Saturday April 25), OPEC+ pre-positioning to flood markets becomes operational planning rather than rhetorical commitment. Goldman $90/bbl Q2 forecast intact under Branch 3 architecture; post-resolution unwind path could test $80-85 within 2 weeks of confirmed Hormuz reopening as the 206K bpd May increase becomes physical. Saudi-Iraq 'covert war' framing (April 12 MoFA summons + UNN/WSJ April 26) over Iran-backed Iraqi militia drone attacks remains active inside OPEC; Iraq + Saudi bilateral friction matters for crude supply discipline at June ministerial. (Sources: Aramco May OSP release Apr 6; Bloomberg refiner survey; Senior Market dossier C5 Branch 3; UNN/WSJ Saudi-Iraq covert-war framing)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED Aramco OSP + structural production data; June ministerial dynamics REPORTED.

signals.content: GOLDMAN INSTITUTIONALIZES 'HIGHER FOR LONGER' AT $90 LATE-2026 BASE CASE; BRANCH 3 PRODUCTION-UNWIND THESIS FALSIFIED [CONFIRMED FORECAST + CONFIRMED OIL TAPE REVERSAL]. CORRECTION: yesterday's $100.47 Brent print was a yfinance contract-roll glitch; live tape Mon $108.23 (+2.75%) / Tue $109.39 (+1.08%). Goldman Sachs raised its Brent forecast for late 2026 to $90/bbl from $80, 'expecting oil prices to stay higher for longer due to more persistent disruptions in the Persian Gulf' (TheStreet, OilPrice, Discovery Alert). Goldman estimated Iran has already curtailed up to 2.5 million barrels per day of crude output in the period leading into late April — a magnitude-of-disruption number larger than mainstream coverage anticipates. The forecast revision INSTITUTIONALIZES a 'higher for longer' oil regime as bank-house view — opposite direction of yesterday's Branch 3 / Russia-mediator de-escalation pricing thesis. The investor-deployment implication is non-trivial: Goldman is now coding the persistent-Hormuz-disruption tail at base case, not tail risk. IEA April Oil Market Report: 'biggest energy supply shock on record.' EIA Short-Term Energy Outlook elevated Brent forecast. OPEC+ pricing restraint signal (Aramco May OSP +$19.50) remains the cleanest Saudi corporate-side bet on resolution within May-June window despite higher spot tape. June ministerial production-unwind decision now runs under continued Brent $108+ regime, not under post-resolution band. (Sources: TheStreet 'Goldman Sachs resets its oil price forecasts for the rest of 2026', OilPrice.com, Fox Business 'Goldman Sachs sees higher inflation due to Iran war oil price shock', IndraStra 'Iran Confronts Imminent Oil Production Cuts', IEA April OMR)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED Goldman forecast revision + CONFIRMED Brent correction + CONFIRMED IEA characterization.

signals.content: UAE LEAVES OPEC AND OPEC+ EFFECTIVE MAY 1 — BIGGEST STRUCTURAL OPEC DEVELOPMENT IN A DECADE [CONFIRMED via 7+ wires]. UAE announced Tuesday April 28 it will leave OPEC and OPEC+ effective May 1, 2026 — ending more than five decades of membership (CONFIRMED — The National UAE [state media], Washington Post 'UAE to leave OPEC amid Hormuz oil crisis', CNBC 'UAE's shock OPEC exit', Al Jazeera, Reuters, Bloomberg, Atlantic Council Apr 28 dispatch, CFR 'Why the UAE Walked Out on OPEC', Fortune, Khaleej Times, Gulf News). UAE rationale: 'more freedom of action to make production decisions without the constraints of OPEC' and pursue 5M bpd capacity by 2027. UAE Energy Minister: timing 'is now because the Strait of Hormuz is closed and the impact of its exit on the oil market will be limited.' Atlantic Council: 'A long time coming.' CRITICAL BESSENT-CONTEXT: UAE Central Bank Governor Balama negotiated dollar-swap-line provisions with Treasury Secretary Bessent DAYS BEFORE this announcement (Fortune confirms the chain). UAE getting consolidated inside American financial architecture; US gets a major producer unshackled-from-OPEC + committed-to-expanded-output. STRUCTURAL REALIGNMENT, NOT QUOTA DISPUTE. Operational implications for OPEC: (a) UAE is second-largest OPEC producer with crucial spare capacity — Saudi Arabia now does more of the heavy lifting on price stability; cartel loses a key shock absorber, (b) Atlantic Council: UAE absence may pose 'existential risk to OPEC sustainability' over 12-24mo, (c) Saudi Arabia views this as direct threat to its leadership of the Gulf region — MBS PUBLIC SILENCE Tue-Wed is itself data (Riyadh's anger, Al Arabiya quiet on UAE-OPEC story = Saudi institutional embarrassment), (d) potential UAE-Saudi rift visible in late-2025 Yemen-related tensions. Atlantic Council/Landon Derentz: 'Now that the UAE won't be beholden to OPEC production quotas, nothing inhibits the country from leveraging its spare capacity in pursuit of its own strategic interests.' UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity within 12-24mo means structural ceiling LOWER medium-term — Goldman's late-2026 $90 base case may be TOO HIGH on the medium-term ceiling. SPLIT-TEMPORAL INTERPRETATION: today's Brent +3.04% to $114.64 (8th straight up session, intraday >$115) priced loss-of-OPEC-shock-absorber as near-term-bearish-for-management (bullish near-term Brent); medium-term unconstrained-UAE-supply is bearish 12-24mo out. Iran SILENT on UAE OPEC exit (cannot publicly object given own market disruption posture); Saudi state-aligned media QUIET on the story (subtle indicator of MBS displeasure). For India: UAE actively pursuing bilateral oil supply with India/China/South Korea/Japan; PSU refiners IOC/BPCL/HPCL already partner ADNOC — Khaleej Times analyst: 'From India's point of view, this is very good development.' (Sources: The National UAE, Washington Post, CNBC, Al Jazeera, Reuters, Bloomberg, Atlantic Council, CFR, Fortune, Khaleej Times, Gulf News, Euronews)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED via UAE state media + 7+ wires + Atlantic Council/CFR think-tank confirmation + Fortune Bessent-chain.

signals.content: UAE OPEC EXIT TAKES EFFECT TOMORROW MAY 1 — CALENDAR INFLECTION; JEDDAH SUMMIT SAUDI-UAE RIFT VISIBLE; SAUDI 3-DAY CONSPICUOUS-QUIET = THRESHOLD CROSSED [CONFIRMED multi-source]. (1) UAE OPEC exit announced Apr 28 takes effect TOMORROW May 1 — ~24 hours away. With UAE OUT, June OPEC+ ministerial decision on production discipline rests on Saudi-Russia-Iraq-Kuwait alone — fundamentally different cartel architecture from previous decades. (2) GCC consultative summit took place in Jeddah Apr 28 chaired by Saudi MBS. UAE sent its FM, NOT its President — VISIBLE DIPLOMATIC SNUB at the host's most important bilateral relationship. UAE Energy Minister Al Mazrouei confirmed UAE did NOT consult Saudi Arabia or any other OPEC country before announcement. (3) Saudi former oil adviser Mohammad al-Sabban publicly downplayed via Al Jazeera ('not a major blow, one country going out doesn't mean anything') — but US News analysis 'UAE Oil Break Exposes Deepening Saudi Rift as Gulf Power Shifts' frames Riyadh as having 'no good public options.' MBS chaired Jeddah but did NOT produce consensus communique on either war or OPEC fracture. Saudi conspicuous-quiet has now persisted Tue-Wed-Thu (3 days), CROSSING THRESHOLD from 'considering response' to 'no good response.' (4) Soufan Center IntelBrief 'Gulf Shakeup.' Atlantic Council 'Petrodollar Crisis' / 'end of Gulf solidarity' (Al Jazeera op-ed). Atlantic Council/Derentz: 'UAE absence may pose existential risk to OPEC sustainability over 12-24mo.' (5) Brent +6-8% to $118-120 on Apr 29 close (Day 1 of $115-sustained CONFIRMED) priced ~20% UAE-OPEC fragmentation contributor; primary driver was Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First (~80%). Tomorrow May 1 is the test: how Saudi/Russia/Iraq/Kuwait coordinate without UAE in the room. (Sources: Al Jazeera Apr 28, The National UAE, US News, Soufan Center, Atlantic Council, Organiser)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED summit attendance + CONFIRMED al-Mazrouei statement + CONFIRMED al-Sabban deflective + CONFIRMED 3-day Saudi conspicuous-quiet + CONFIRMED Brent +6-8% Apr 29 close.

edges.context: Day 62 Wednesday. UAE OPEC exit effective May 1 fundamentally repriced cartel architecture: loss of shock-absorber capacity = near-term-bullish-Brent (today +3.04% to $114.64), but medium-term UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity = structural ceiling lower 12-24mo out (medium-term-bearish-Brent). Goldman $90 late-2026 base case may be TOO HIGH on medium-term ceiling per Atlantic Council/Derentz. Aramco May OSP +$19.50/bbl premium pricing restraint signal still operative. June ministerial now runs under different cartel architecture without UAE in the room.

Day 60 Monday. Aramco May OSP +$19.50/bbl premium = pricing restraint. Saudi -23% MoM (10.1 → 7.8 mbpd). Yanbu bypass at 4.2M bpd capacity. Saudi-Iraq 'covert war' framing inside OPEC active. Branch 3 makes Saudi pricing restraint operational — preparing for Hormuz partial reopening / June ministerial unwind.

Day 62 Wednesday. Hormuz still functionally closed Day 62; war-risk insurance ~1% AWRP (US DFC $40B reinsurance backstop capping); Brent breached $115 intraday. UAE OPEC exit effective May 1 = the structural realignment is being triggered BY the Hormuz disruption (UAE Energy Minister: 'timing is now because Strait of Hormuz is closed'). Cartel architecture and chokepoint architecture mutually reinforcing.

UAE leaves OPEC and OPEC+ effective May 1, 2026 (CONFIRMED Apr 28). Atlantic Council: existential risk to OPEC sustainability over 12-24mo. Saudi MBS public silence is data of Saudi anger. Loss of UAE as second-largest producer + crucial spare capacity = OPEC shock-absorber capacity diminished. Bessent dollar-swap-line negotiated days before signals UAE inside US financial architecture. New edge — first-order structural realignment.

trigger_points.condition: OPEC+ June ministerial announces production unwind
trigger_points.mechanism: Under Branch 3 architecture (Russia-mediated 4-8 week resolution; Brent gap -4.6% to $100.47 Apr 27), the June OPEC+ ministerial meeting becomes the operational forum for production-unwind decisions. Aramco May OSP +$19.50/bbl premium (vs Bloomberg refiner survey expected $40+) signals pricing restraint and Aramco preparation for Hormuz partial reopening. A formal OPEC+ June announcement of production-unwind activation (extending the 206K bpd May increase, OR a larger Saudi/Russia coordinated supply commitment) would: (a) accelerate Brent path to $80-85 within 2 weeks; (b) compress US 10Y inflation expectations; (c) free Fed for June dovish-pivot; (d) reverse the energy-crisis-hedge complex (Cheniere, Frontline, BASF) further. Falsifiable: OPEC+ June ministerial communiqué text. Watching: Saudi-Iraq friction inside OPEC could complicate the unwind announcement; Russia-Saudi alignment under Russia-mediated architecture is the supportive factor.

trigger_points.condition: Saudi Arabia issues formal public response to UAE OPEC exit within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)
trigger_points.mechanism: MBS / Saudi state has been conspicuously silent Tue-Wed on UAE OPEC exit. Atlantic Council: Riyadh will 'view this action as a new direct threat.' Saudi institutional embarrassment is signaled by Al Arabiya quietness. Activation criterion: confirmed Saudi formal statement (MBS, MoFA, Aramco, OPEC delegation) addressing UAE departure. Falsifiable: 2026-05-12 23:59 IST. ADDED 2026-04-29 at watching.

trigger_points.condition: Second OPEC member announces departure within 90 days of UAE exit (cartel-disintegration cascade)
trigger_points.mechanism: Atlantic Council framing: UAE absence may pose existential risk to OPEC sustainability over 12-24mo. Most-watched candidates: Iraq (Saudi-Iraq covert war + quota friction), Kazakhstan (long-stretching capacity), Nigeria (chronic underproduction). A second departure would convert UAE exit from idiosyncratic event to cartel-disintegration trajectory and crash Brent's medium-term ceiling further. Falsifiable: 2026-07-29 23:59 IST. ADDED 2026-04-29 at watching.

current.saudi_position: carrying more burden post-UAE; MBS publicly silent; needs higher revenue post-Sadara damage
